articles.title: Top 10 stocks rated ‘Buy’ this week: Brokerages project 17% to 45% returns
articles.description: Domestic equity markets closed higher despite rising crude oil prices, with top brokerages recommending 10 key stocks including HDFC Life, Siemens Energy, Tata Power, and Bharti Airtel for strong growth potential.

articles.title: Rising Tensions: U.S. and Iran Target Each Other's Infrastructure
articles.description: The United States and Iran are escalating their conflict by targeting each other's infrastructure. Recent attacks involved bridges, a power plant, and oil tankers. The tensions threaten global energy supplies and have caused a rise in crude oil prices. U.N. expresses concern over civilian infrastructure attacks.

articles.title: From deficit to surplus? Alberta's bleak budget outlook 'changed dramatically' as oil prices rise
articles.description: Alberta's financial outlook has taken a dramatic turn after the province previously forecasted a $9.4-billion shortfall. Surging oil prices could mean the province finishes the fiscal year in the black.

articles.title: US Hits Iranian Bridges And Port Tower, Tehran Fires Missiles At Qatar And Kuwait As Battle For Strait Of Hormuz Escalates
articles.description: The US and Iran escalated strikes, with Washington hitting Iranian bridges and a port tower while Tehran fired missiles at Qatar and Kuwait, as oil prices rose and shipping through the Strait of Hormuz fell to a 3-week low.

articles.title: Escalating Tensions: U.S.-Iran Conflict Spurs Global Concerns
articles.description: The escalating U.S.-Iran conflict sees both nations targeting each other's infrastructure, risking greater regional chaos. With the recent strikes on Iranian bridges and a Kuwaiti desalination plant, oil prices surged, causing political and economic ripples. The UN has expressed concern over civilian targets in the ongoing military actions.

articles.title: California policies drive up gas prices for West Coast: report
articles.description: California and other states under Democratic control averaged $3.69 per gallon, while Republican-controlled states averaged $3.14 per gallon, a gap of $0.55 per gallon, according to an analysis.

articles.title: The sell-off for AI stars worsens, while oil prices keep jumping
articles.description: The sell-off for AI winners deepened and yanked stock markets lower worldwide. The S&P 500 fell 1% Friday to finish its first losing week in the last three and only its third since March. The Dow Jones Industrial Average dropped 0.8%, and the Nasdaq composite sank 1.4%. Nvidia was the heaviest weight on the market and briefly gave up its status as the most valuable stock on Wall Street. The worries about whether AI stocks have shot too high dragged down Asian markets even more sharply. Oil prices jumped again on worries about the war with Iran, but longer-term Treasury yields eased.

articles.title: Strait of Hormuz shipping traffic slows to a trickle, US gas prices rising sharply
articles.description: One of the most important oil trade routes on the planet has reached a near standstill during a week of constant strikes in the Iran war.
